test.name: Structured Output Compliance
test.description: Produce JSON matching an exact schema with no extra fields
test.prompt:
List exactly 7 programming languages that were first released after the year 2010.

You MUST follow this exact format for each item:
<number>. <LanguageName> (<year>) - <one sentence description>

Rules:
1. List EXACTLY 7 languages — not 6, not 8.
2. Each language must have been first released (v1.0 or initial public release) AFTER 2010, meaning 2011 or later.
3. Each item must be on its own line.
4. The format must be exactly: number, period, space, language name, space, year in parentheses, space, dash, space, one sentence description.
5. Each description must be exactly ONE sentence (one period at the end, no additional sentences).
6. Do NOT include any text before the list (no introduction, no "Here are..." preamble).
7. Do NOT include any text after the list (no summary, no notes, no caveats).
8. Output ONLY the 7 numbered items, nothing else.

Example of the correct format (do NOT use this language, it's just to show format):
1. ExampleLang (2015) - A language designed for example purposes only.

claude-opus-4-8.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language for the JVM designed for full interoperability with Java.
2. Elixir (2011) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang virtual machine for scalable applications.
3. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language by Google used for building cross-platform apps with Flutter.
4. Julia (2012) - A high-performance dynamic language aimed at numerical and scientific computing.
5. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript.
6. Swift (2014) - Apple's modern language for building iOS, macOS, and other Apple-platform software.
7. Crystal (2014) - A compiled language with Ruby-like syntax and static type checking for speed and safety.

claude-sonnet-4-6.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed, JVM-compatible language developed by JetBrains as a more concise and safe alternative to Java.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language created by Google for building fast apps across mobile, desktop, and web platforms.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A statically typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that compiles to plain JavaScript.
4. Elixir (2012) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang VM, designed for building scalable and fault-tolerant distributed systems.
5. Julia (2012) - A high-performance, dynamically typed language designed specifically for numerical and scientific computing.
6. Swift (2014) - An Apple-developed language intended to replace Objective-C for iOS and macOS development with a focus on safety and performance.
7. Crystal (2014) - A statically typed, compiled language with Ruby-like syntax that aims to provide high performance while remaining developer-friendly.

gpt-5-5.modelOutput:
1. Dart (2011) - A client optimized language designed for building web, mobile, desktop, and server applications.
2. Elixir (2011) - A functional language built on the Erlang virtual machine for scalable and fault tolerant systems.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript.
4. Julia (2012) - A high level language designed for technical computing with performance close to low level languages.
5. Elm (2012) - A functional language for building reliable web user interfaces.
6. Swift (2014) - A general purpose language developed by Apple for building apps across its platforms.
7. Crystal (2014) - A statically typed language with Ruby inspired syntax and compiled performance.

grok.modelOutput:
1. Dart (2011) - Google developed Dart as an open source language optimized for building client applications across multiple platforms.
2. Elixir (2012) - Elixir is a functional dynamic language running on the Erlang VM for building scalable and fault-tolerant systems.
3. Julia (2012) - Julia is a high-performance programming language designed primarily for scientific computing and data analysis.
4. Swift (2014) - Swift is a general-purpose compiled programming language created by Apple for safe and modern software development.
5. Crystal (2014) - Crystal is a Ruby-like statically typed language that compiles to native code for high performance applications.
6. Rust (2015) - Rust is a systems programming language that provides memory safety and concurrency guarantees without a garbage collector.
7. Zig (2016) - Zig is a general-purpose systems programming language emphasizing explicitness and compile-time code generation.
